item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of scips where a GOI mocks the foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Meta-articles irk me, as do articles that serve as some sort of "take that" to a person/trend/site/cookbook that the author dislikes. This was creepy and kind of cool, but the whole "lol bad writers are bad at writing" tone left a bad taste in my mouth.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Inner jokes and "fuck you, casuals, leave our SCP alone, we are so speshul"" butthurt. Oh, God, why. Downvoting it so hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it and am upvoting, but it causes me great pain to not get what the reference that apparently exists in the article is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I read this, and I don't know anything about whatever this is based on. It still works (maybe works better). I wasn't sure if the was originally a Foundation site that got corrupted by multiple anomalies therein or some kind of chameleon. Either way works, though. I also like that the Foundation response was basically "Nope".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still don't know what it is. I'm too new.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it, i was wrong about what it is and its still great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm terribly sorry. I forgot at the time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Instead of replying to your own comments, edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nevermind… I found it. I love that D-Class are now super SPESHUL.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, for clarification, is the rp on wikidot? I ask so I can know for sure I'm looking at the correct train wreck right now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Judging from the discussion, I was way off, but reading over this, it felt like this was something the O5s fucked up and wanted to bury, like they'd created something, and it just wanted to be like them and make them proud, bringing them presents and such, creating these anomalous objects and bringing them like a cat brings you dead birds and mice to make you happy. The fact that the pamphlets were listed last, and the example mysteriously destroyed, made me think that they actually did work, that O5 had taken them for personal use and then burned the evidence. Honestly, finding out the real idea behind this was a bit of a letdown, but not enough to prevent an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not knowing the meta side to it, I assumed it was sort of a chameleon building - it takes cues from what's placed in it - and when the Foundation found it, it decided it rather liked them and decided to be like them. …so, pretty much like a normal building roleplaying as a Foundation site, now that I think. Wow. That's sort of genius.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yup, I don't put anybody's rights for voicing opinions away. But expecting that a phenomenon like this will not spread onto the mainstream culture (whatever that even is), but it does, people are entertained in a good way because of it (thus why I mentioned the T-shirts with "rage faces" on them, seeing a parallel with the RP site) and so - going about like a crazy schoolgirl ridiculing them, that doesn't make much sense to me. One would expect we wouldn't even care as the relatively and supposedly mature and knowledgable ones (I am using "we", because it's weird to suddenly estrange myself from the rest of the commenters and writers here). While I don't know if that was the reason Sorts made this SCP, the thing I see from some of the reactions are some seriously rustled jimmies. Like - Yeah, we sure showed them!
If anybody comes to the site with an application consisting of "hurr durr I know the secret of 087", it's a sure way to get fired anyway.
_
I dunno, I guess the overall quality of the SCP probably didn't do its' full job for me because I wasn't really on the joke. As for the idea with the changing facial features, well, that's another (and good!) story.
_
Thanks for the link, btw. Worth a read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Refer to this thread for insight on what the community think about the introduction of the contents of this wiki on mainstream culture.

As for the article, this is indeed based in a low quality RP about the Foundation. Whether that makes us "hipsters" or "elitists", I do not know nor care, but I believe that the people putting time and effort into writing good articles are entitled the right of voicing their opinions regarding particularly bad "parodies", just like any person in the internet.

Sorts did it in a particularly clever manner, which works whether or not you actually know the source.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
None of the above applies. It's a mockery/what which tries to imitate the Foundation.

It tries so hard guys but it doesn't quite get it ;n;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If this is a parody on a cosplay/roleplay/anythingmadebyfansbutnotreallyconflictingwiththissitehere site, this is unneededly (is this even a word?) elitist and not really funny. It reminds me of the time when meme faces were first printed on t-shirts and people on funnyjunk went mad. "Hey, we are the cool kids that have memes, not you!"

If this is based on the old foundation, I admit I don't get it. But… that was Gears, right?

If nothing of the above applies, this is quite an interesting SCP. The shifting faces manual… hmm.

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ditto.

I think it should require feeding, unless it's just that weird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: http://www.freakingnews.com/pi-cruise-Pictures-56668.asp

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a joke I'm not getting. How are face-altering pamphlets supposed to creep me out again?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That Sorts guy… I hear he grew a beard and is still here, but that's a damn lie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I see this whole thing as a twisted allegory for expungements and interpretations.

To a certain extent, the expungements serve to "wipe out" the effects or details of a particular SCP from the article's narrative. The known attributes of an object may hint at what those details might be, but it is not always readily apparent.

Thus, the objects stored in this complex do not explicitly have any anomalous attributes despite being contained as SCPs.

However, in lieu of the SCPs not having those expunged anomalies spelled out, a reader must individually come up with the details in his/her own imagination. The people with accounts here usually end up publicly sharing their interpretations on the discussion page. Many times each person's interpretation conflicts with those of others, and sometimes a single person may personally rattle off multiple contradictory theories on the expunged details to see which one(s) stick(s). And every so often, one of us may write up a Tale that gives a more in-depth look at one's theory/theories and how it would gel with the SCP.

Thus, the humanoid entities in this complex are themselves the ones with the discernible anomalous attributes, such as the constantly morphing faces, that are informed by what is known about the objects they are containing.

The faulty but detailed knowledge about the Foundation, as well as the use of ineffective tactical gear and improvised weaponry, is meant to represent those times when the theories are formed based on either a.) an incomplete knowledge of the universe/Guides of the SCP Foundation, or b.) an attempt to include "bad-ass" elements or other storytelling elements that clash with the tone and purpose of the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't really have any knowledge of that RP site before I read the article, and it still worked for me, like reality farted (indeed, like the record skipped) and a not-quite-right Foundation appeared. I got it, and when the references and the parody elements were explained to me it made it even better, but didn't diminish my initial impression one bit. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Take my upvotes, all of them

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this regardless of any reference to any RP site. I like it for the same reason I like Scantron's 001 proposal: it's a vision of a Foundation that just isn't quite right. The reference to the uncanny valley made earlier in the discussion sums it up nicely. It's humorous in a way that's still disquieting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… but… i didn't write this…

Now that Sorts guy

:3

YOU SAW NOTHING!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reject is a pretty cool guy.

Now that Sorts guy…I dunno about him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … but… i didn't write this…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't believe this actually exists.

Sorts you're a bad bad bad bad man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some the items sound familiar. Is this whole skip a gentle rib on some bad SCP's?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My problem with this is that, to me, it appears to be a massive "take that" to crappy fanfiction & bad cosplay in general, and SCP fanfic in specific. I see in the comments that this was apparently partially inspired by such a fanfic/RP site, which I find somewhat disappointing. The whole thing reads more like a cosplay/LARP of the Foundation that got transported to the Foundation-verse. With melty faces.

"SCPs are real, man! I found one in my cookie jar and I want to join your site to write about it!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed the read, it was interesting enough to keep my attention, upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Agent Redacted" is the only remaining cool kid reference (well, that and Agents D and X trying to talk to him).

When this was more of a joke it was about some agents being turned into a bunch of 16 year-old 25 year-olds (not a typo) who dual wielded scythes and some other agents being swallowed up by that madness. And yeah, that's way too in-jokey which is why none of that was included in the final draft. This was supposed to be less about the other site than people realize, but I suppose that's what I get for including anything about them at all.

Some of the stuff that remains is absurd, but it's absurd for other reasons:

There's a face pamphlet and now everyone looks like a picasso
There's a DYI book and now everyone's obsessed with power tools
There's a silly suit of useless armor and everyone is trapped in silly, useless armor
There's an old record player and all records of the site and its contents are almost entirely corrupted or impossible to keep

There are some intentionally hidden references in here, like all of the gears on Dr Steampunk are a callback to clockwork themed SCPs/Dr Gears, and the lightbulbs are a shout out to a previous attempt at a corrupted SCP site that helped inspire this, but a lot of the other stuff is just in there to be weird or mysterious and let people find patterns or explanations beyond the ones I intended.

The 1990 music CDs were found in 1950. That's my headcanon. >_>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not in on "the joke", but I like this on its own merits. +1'd!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The expungement was about an entirely different SCP (which the entity just happened to know about), and wasn't an expungement of the SCP itself, so I don't think there should be a problem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just to be clear, let's not link to them and cause more of a shitstorm than we already have.

…and it's probably not a good idea for any of us who've made an account there to link this to them either. That would not go over well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I immediately flashed to those towns in russia during the cold war that were mocked up to look like American communities so they could train their spies. And upvoted on the basis of that alone even without knowing anything about this "forum".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not familiar with whatever RP forum people are talking about, and this is so meta it hurts, but I somehow still like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *sob* *tears* I just wanna be cool like you guys!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah! Hey Sorts, are you sure there isn't an AWCY? connection just waiting to be made?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh. Wow! I mean, the face pamphlets are definitely the one really creepy aspect to this (especially since they're not related to , but I didn't get that at all, specifically that they'd lost personnel inside this.

Mostly I just like how much this reminds me of Skip, but on a facility scale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there a link to the forum? I am unfamiliar with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like the lost Foundation site angle could have been played straighter without all the cool-kid references. I mean hell, such an idea could actually merit a meta-narrative, which are extremely difficult to pull off. As it stands now, I think the "hey guys I totally wrote this thing up" feeling I'm getting here is getting in the way of something that could be really awesome. I'm just not understanding why any joke parts were necessary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oy vey, this is terrible, and I don't mean that in the jokey giggles oh this is wrong but I love it way, I mean it's just bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am really conflicted on this. I dislike the meta-aspect of this, but love the idea of a Foundation Site turned into a skip (which happened before, but I think the concept is too good to only have one of it). Even just with the entities, I really like some parts of them (bi-location, asymmetric faces) while disliking others (skin-clothes, improvised weaponry).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: that was one of the original draft ideas, so it's not a bad interpretation

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh. I was thinking that the fake-Site was an entity trying to emulate the Foundation, and failing miserably at it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: apart from a guy taking out fifty zombies with a power drill being ridiculous that's not supposed to be a joke, it's supposed to be a clue that this was an actual Foundation site once :<

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This isn't entirely a joke. I mean, I tried to take as much of the joke parts out as I could. This is a knock on some of the things that go on at the terrible RP forum but there's also ideas that I've been working over for actual horror purposes, like the face pamphlet.

Basically, those non-anomalous items all used to have actual properties which were ultimately absorbed by the site itself. The face pamphlets are the obvious one, but the big problem is that its nigh impossible to keep accurate records about certain aspects of the site itself. When O5 realized that anyone they assigned to contain this thing would be forgotten they bricked it over and left everyone inside to their fate. The people inside lost their faces and their histories.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be honest, I'm pretty sure you are in fact missing a joke here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The SCP in question is SCP-008, which is supposed to be for level 4 clearance only, which is why it was blanked out here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't even like in-joke meta stuff in -Js, let alone mainlist articles. This is well written, but I have to downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooooh. I figured that it was "there is no canon" in SCP form, because of the constant shifts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This.

Is.

WONDERFUL.

An outer epidermis that resembles elaborate but functionless tactical gear. While some portions of this epidermis can be removed, doing so improperly causes great pain or distress to the entity

"NOOO DON'T TAKE IT OFF ME THE GLUE FOR THE FOAM JUST FINISHED DRYIIIIIING I SPENT ALL MY TIP MONEY TO MAKE THIS FOR SCPCON"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pssst: the joke is that this is based on a rather low-quality RP fansite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorts… you magnificent bastard I read your BOOK!

Well not really, but I would if you wrote one. When I realized exactly what this was about I couldn't help but upvote. Good show.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1870 entity: [DATA EXPUNGED]

This pisses me off like nothing else, since I despise expungement, even with the O5 thing and the fact this expungement is fairly plausible here. I just can't take it at all seriously. I feel like I'm missing a joke I don't understand. So I'm largely not going to vote on this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You and me both. I feel like I'm missing out on some gigantic inside joke. My best guess is that all of the "SCPs" listed are from deleted articles… I recognize the lightbulbs, and think there was a credenza-mounted record player that recently got baleeted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But no one winged angel?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did you… did you actually…
